SURVEY CONFIRMED EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 109 MPH. 2.6 MILE PATH LENGTH BEGAN JUST EAST OF RICKENBACKER AFB AND EXTENDED TO LANCASTER ROAD. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

A SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 83 MPH AT A GREENHOUSE COMPLEX. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 250 YARDS AND THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. TWELVE GREENHOUSES WERE DAMAGED. BALLED AND BURLAPPED TREES AND PLANT TRANSPORT CARTS WERE PULLED SOUTH TOWARD THE CENTER LINE OF THE TORNADO TRACK. PREVAILING WINDS WITH THE ASSOCIATED SQUALL LINE WERE FROM THE WEST-SOUTHWEST.

A SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.2 MILES AND THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 25 YARDS. THE TORNADO DESTROYED A GARAGE ON FOLSOM-JONESVILLE ROAD, AND ALSO DAMAGED A TRAILER AND ANOTHER NEARBY HOUSE. WHEN IT CROSSED WARSAW ROAD TO THE EAST, IT DESTROYED A BARN.
